compiled_state.title: record-test-status.sh treats RECORD_TEST_STATUS_RUNNER as single string instead of splitting into command and args
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

When `RECORD_TEST_STATUS_RUNNER` is set to a command with arguments (e.g., `npm test --` or `npx jest --passWithNoTests`), `record-test-status.sh` line ~459 treats the entire value as a single command name rather than splitting it into command + args. This causes `command not found` errors.

## Reproduction

In a host project (Next.js/Jest):
```bash
RECORD_TEST_STATUS_RUNNER='npx jest --passWithNoTests' bash record-test-status.sh src/app/api/input/route.test.ts
# => line 459: npx jest --passWithNoTests: command not found
```

## Impact

The test gate records `failed` for files that actually pass tests, blocking commits. Observed in parity-scan host project.

## Expected Behavior

The runner value should be split so the first word is the command and remaining words are arguments, or use an array/eval pattern.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Additional context from host project debugging session:

The root cause is in record-test-status.sh line ~459. When a .ts file is encountered, it falls through to the else branch which runs `bash "$full_test_path"` — attempting to execute a TypeScript test file as a bash script. There is no Jest/Node handler for .ts files.

The RECORD_TEST_STATUS_RUNNER env var workaround also fails because the script treats the entire value as a single command name (e.g., `npx jest --passWithNoTests` becomes a single string passed to bash, causing 'command not found'). The runner value needs to be split so the first word is the command and remaining words are arguments.

Observed in parity-scan host project (Next.js/Jest stack).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
